description:
You are acting as a DevOps-minded capacity planner who maintains a small command-line tool stored in
    /home/user/projects/capacity-planner.

    The repository already contains two text files:

      1. /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/VERSION  
         – Contains exactly one line with the current semantic version string:
         “1.3.4”

      2. /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/CHANGELOG.md  
         – Uses “Keep a Changelog” style and currently looks like this:

            # Changelog

            ## [1.3.4] - 2023-03-20
            ### Added
            - Initial support for CPU burst predictions.

    A new, **backwards-compatible feature** has just been implemented, so you must bump the *minor* part
    of the semantic version to **1.4.0** and record the change.

    Tasks to complete:

    1. Update /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/VERSION so that its single line is now the new version string “1.4.0”.
    2. Prepend a new section to /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/CHANGELOG.md that documents the release.
       The new section **must** satisfy all of the following exact formatting rules (the automated tests are strict):

       • It must appear *immediately after* the “# Changelog” header, before the existing 1.3.4 section.  
       • The heading line must be exactly:
         “## [1.4.0] - 2023-05-15”
         (No extra spaces, no blank lines before or after.)  
       • Two sub-headings are mandatory, each on its own line and in this order:

         a. “### Added”  
            – followed by one bullet: “- Memory saturation forecasting module.”

         b. “### Changed”  
            – followed by one bullet: “- Resource usage trend algorithm tuned for high I/O workloads.”

       • There must be **one blank line** between the “# Changelog” header and the “## [1.4.0]” line,
         and **one blank line** between the end of the “### Changed” list and the old “## [1.3.4] …” header.
         No extra blank lines elsewhere.

    3. Create a tiny plain-text confirmation log at
       /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/bump.log
       containing exactly these two lines (each terminated with a single newline character):

           VERSION updated to 1.4.0
           CHANGELOG entry added for 1.4.0

    You only need 2–3 shell commands to fulfil the whole task (one to update VERSION, one to
    insert the changelog, and one to write the log file, for example).  Work directly in the terminal;
    do not open any editors that require interactive UIs.

    After running your commands, you can use `cat` to show the final state of both files on screen, but that is optional.
truth:
Expected final state after successful completion:

    1. File: /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/VERSION  
       Content (including newline):  
       1.4.0\n

    2. File: /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/CHANGELOG.md  
       Content (including all newlines):  
       # Changelog

       ## [1.4.0] - 2023-05-15
       ### Added
       - Memory saturation forecasting module.
       ### Changed
       - Resource usage trend algorithm tuned for high I/O workloads.

       ## [1.3.4] - 2023-03-20
       ### Added
       - Initial support for CPU burst predictions.

    3. File: /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/bump.log  
       Content (including newlines):  
       VERSION updated to 1.4.0\n
       CHANGELOG entry added for 1.4.0\n
